title: What is a CV Dataset?
concept: A computer vision dataset is a collection of images and annotations used to train and test vision models.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the primary purpose of dataset annotations?

options.text: They provide ground-truth labels (like classes or coordinates) for training and evaluation
They compress the raw image files to save disk space
They automatically speed up model inference latency on edge devices

exercises.explain: Annotations are the labels or coordinates that tell the model what exists in each image, acting as the answer key.

cards.title: A Library of Pixels
cards.body: Just like a child learns to recognize animals by looking at picture books, a computer vision model learns by studying millions of labeled images. In this unit, we explore how standardized image libraries shape the history and future of AI.

cards.title: Computer Vision Dataset
cards.body: A Computer Vision Dataset consists of raw visual files (images or video) paired with metadata annotations. These annotations dictate what objects are present, where they are, and how they behave.

cards.title: Textbooks with Answer Keys
cards.body: Imagine preparing for a big exam. The raw images are like the practice questions, and the annotations are the answer keys at the back of the book. Without the answer key, you can't verify if you solved the problem correctly.

cards.title: Standardized Collections
cards.body: Popular datasets like ImageNet or COCO standardize their formats. Instead of engineers collecting random web photos, they download one balanced, labeled folder of files, ensuring everyone evaluates models on the same test.

cards.title: Bigger is not always better
cards.body: You might think a dataset of 1 billion poor-quality, mislabeled web images is better than 1 million clean, human-verified images. In reality, noisy labels and poor variety degrade model performance, making curation key.

cards.title: Training vs. Testing Sets
cards.body: Dividing the dataset is critical for honest evaluation:

left.text:
Used by the model to adjust weights.
Largest portion of data.
Analogy: Homework study guides.

right.text:
Used to evaluate final performance.
Strictly hidden during training.
Analogy: The final exam.

exercises.prompt: What are the two main components of a computer vision dataset?
exercises.explain: A dataset contains the raw visual files alongside the annotations that label the content of those files.

exercises.prompt: True or False: The testing set should be used during model training to help adjust weight parameters.
exercises.explain: False. The testing set must remain strictly hidden during training to ensure the final evaluation is unbiased.
Standard benchmarks ensure everyone tests models under identical conditions to measure progress.
exercises.sentence: Standardized datasets allow researchers to compare different models on the same ___ scale.

exercises.prompt: Which of these is a key function of dataset annotations?
exercises.explain: Annotations serve as ground-truth labels that guide the learning and validation process.

options.text: Providing the ground-truth answers for the model to learn from
Increasing the frame rate of real-time cameras
Adding convolutional layers to a neural network

exercises.prompt: Why is it dangerous to train a model and evaluate it on the exact same set of images?

options.text: The model will memorize the images, giving a falsely high evaluation score that does not reflect its real-world performance.
The loss function will become impossible to compute on the GPU.
The images will become corrupted and unreadable by python.
The learning rate will automatically decay to zero immediately.

exercises.explain: Evaluating on the training data causes overfitting/memorization, hiding the model's inability to generalize to new images.
